dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses rolling mean of log returns across multiple lookback periods (15, 30, 60, 120 days). Entry conditions based on sign of rolling mean: positive log_returns indicates uptrend (long), negative indicates downtrend (short). Exit when sign flips. The rolling mean calculation is implicit in the log_returns indicator evaluation across the specified lookback windows. Position sizing is equal-weighted across all four momentum horizons as specified.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses rolling mean of log returns across multiple lookback periods (15, 30, 60, 120 days). Entry conditions based on sign of rolling mean: positive log_returns indicates uptrend (long), negative indicates downtrend (short). Exit when sign flips. The rolling mean calculation is implicit in the log_returns indicator evaluation across the specified lookback windows. Position sizing is equal-weighted across all four momentum horizons as specified.

source_rule_text: Generate trading signals by taking the sign of the rolling mean of returns over varying lookback periods, allowing simultaneous testing of multiple momentum horizons.
